originalWorkflowJson.description: Automated workflow: Google Sheet to Mailchimp. This workflow processes data and performs automated tasks.
originalWorkflowJson.notes: Excellent quality workflow: Google Sheet to Mailchimp. This workflow has been optimized for production use with comprehensive error handling, security, and documentation.

prerequisites: An active n8n instance installed locally, in a Docker container, or hosted on n8n Cloud.
Basic familiarity with importing JSON templates in n8n.
setupInstructions: Download the JSON configuration for this workflow.
Ensure you set up credentials for all connected integrations.
Test your trigger and run execution.
configurationSteps: Verify the settings of the trigger node "On clicking 'execute'".
Open the "Google Sheets" Google Sheets node.
Ensure you select the spreadsheet file and specific tab/sheet from the dropdown menus.
Map the input JSON keys to your target sheet columns.
Open the "Mailchimp" node (n8n-nodes-base.mailchimp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Interval" node (n8n-nodes-base.interval) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Error Handler" node (n8n-nodes-base.stopAndError) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
No global variables node (Set node) is present. If you need dynamic constants, add a Set node after the trigger.
testingSteps: Click the "Test step" or "Listen for test event" button on your Trigger node.
Send a test trigger from your source service (e.g. fill a test form or trigger a webhook trigger).
Verify that the node canvas lights up green, indicating successful data flow.
Open the final destination node to confirm that output records were saved correctly.
instructions.expectedResult: Upon trigger activation, inputs will flow through the configured nodes and execute actions successfully.
troubleshooting: Check credential warning symbols. Most workflow issues stem from expired OAuth connections or missing API permissions.
Verify that the data keys coming from your Trigger node match the exact names mapped in subsequent nodes (e.g., {{ $json.body.email }}).
Open the Execution history tab in n8n to view logs and determine which node failed.
securityNotes: Never hardcode API keys, passwords, or authentication bearer tokens in the plain text fields of node parameters.
If you download or share this workflow JSON, sanitize any sensitive parameters or configuration nodes.
customizationIdeas: Add an email node at the end to notify you of successful executions.
Incorporate an AI LLM node to summarize trigger payloads before writing them to integrations.
